description: A designed promotional graphic built over a soft-focus stock photograph of two people embracing in what appears to be an airport terminal (blurred travelers and ceiling lights in the background). One person, wearing a denim jacket and a face mask and carrying a shoulder bag, hugs another person dressed in a white sleeved top; the scene reads as an emotional reunion or farewell at a departure gate. Over the left/lower portion of the image, white sans-serif text reads: 'If you self-deport now, you MAY be able to return as an immigrant or visitor someday —' followed by a very large serif headline 'the LEGAL way' where the word 'LEGAL' is rendered in bright green for emphasis. A dark blue band across the bottom carries the DHS seal and the large 'ICE' wordmark on the left, and on the right: 'Learn more at: ICE.gov/self-deportation'. The propaganda technique pairs a warm, hopeful human image (reunion, return) with the coercive 'self-deport now' message, using the color-highlighted word 'LEGAL' to frame voluntary departure as the path to legitimate future return. Target audience: undocumented immigrants, encouraging voluntary departure via the CBP Home / self-deportation program.
notable_text: If you self-deport now, you MAY be able to return as an immigrant or visitor someday — the LEGAL way; Learn more at: ICE.gov/self-deportation
